title: hieracium murorum subsp. symphacelodes
side_effects: Hieracium murorum subsp. symphacelodes can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its saponin and sesquiterpene lactone content. Additionally, excessive consumption may lead to allergic reactions, such as skin irritation and respiratory problems. In severe cases, improper use of the plant may result in liver and kidney damage, as well as interact negatively with certain medications. Prolonged exposure to its toxic compounds can also cause headaches, dizziness, and fatigue.
